titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the World War II servicemen alumni files, class years 1939-1945.
World War II servicemen alumni files
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Eileen Keating

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

profiledesc.creation: Finding aid encoded by Rebecca Keating, August 2008

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the World War II Servicemen Alumni Files, 1939-1945.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu
http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.unittitle: World War II servicemen alumni files,
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Correspondence, photographs, clippings, and biographical data concerning Cornell University alumni who died during World War II.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, photographs, clippings, and biographical data concerning Cornell University alumni who died during World War II.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: For all other alumni, see Cornell deceased alumni files, #41-2-877.

did.unittitle: Bakewell, Chief Warrant Officer Joseph Hunter
Brokenshire, Lt. Comdr. Herbert Cecil Grad.
2nd Lt. Howard W. Eggert SP. AGR
Kearns, 2nd Lt. Charles MI Chael, Jr. AUS
Ma Kirdy, Col. Howard Spencer Med.
Morgan, Sgt. George Alexander Sp. Agr.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: World War II servicemen alumni files, #41-2-822. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
